title: euphorbia crassimarginata
intro: Euphorbia crassimarginata, also known as the 'Thick-Margined Spurge', is a succulent plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's sap and roots are often used to create tinctures, infusions, and creams, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
